response: In Verona, feuding Montague and Capulet clans’ teen heirs—Romeo (Montague) and Juliet (Capulet)—fall instantly for each other at a masked ball, wed secretly via Friar Laurence, who hopes to mend their grudge. When Tybalt slays Romeo’s friend Mercutio, Romeo kills Tybalt and is exiled; the friar’s fake-death ruse for Juliet backfires, leaving both dead, their families finally reconciling.
